original_abstract: In this biennial review of atomic-weight determinations and related isotopic data, we present revised standard atomic weights for five chemical elements. Significant updates include the reclassification of bromine from 79.904(1) to the interval [79.901, 79.907] and magnesium from 24.3050(6) to [24.304, 24.307]. These shifts to interval notation reflect the natural variations in isotopic abundance found across common terrestrial materials. Furthermore, the standard atomic weights for germanium, indium, and mercury have been refined to 72.630(8), 114.818(1), and 200.592(3), respectively, providing greater precision for the scientific community.
humanized_abstract: This Biennial Review summarizes recent developments in atomic weight determinations and in associated aspects of isotopic data. Revised standard atomic weights are recommended for five elements, bromine on account of the adoption of a new definition and representation of its atomic weight, including margins of variation of the naturally occurring mixtures of its bromine isotopes found in present samples in common terrestrial materials; and magnesium, germanium, indium, and mercury based on improvements in determinations of their mean atomic weights.

document.input: In this biennial review of atomic-weight determinations and related isotopic data, we present revised standard atomic weights for five chemical elements. Significant updates include the reclassification of bromine from 79.904(1) to the interval [79.901, 79.907] and magnesium from 24.3050(6) to [24.304, 24.307]. These shifts to interval notation reflect the natural variations in isotopic abundance found across common terrestrial materials. Furthermore, the standard atomic weights for germanium, indium, and mercury have been refined to 72.630(8), 114.818(1), and 200.592(3), respectively, providing greater precision for the scientific community.
